lesson: c2-l09 / Building Simple Web Tools with Lovable
title: Building Simple Web Tools with Lovable

scenes.name: Title
scenes.text: Building simple web tools with <emphasis>Lovable</emphasis>. [pause] Describe an app in plain English — get a working web tool back.

scenes.name: What it is
scenes.text: Lovable is an online builder. [pause] You write what the tool should do. It generates a working web app. You refine it through conversation.

scenes.name: Where it lives
scenes.text: Sign up at <emphasis>lovable.dev</emphasis>. [pause] Chat window on the left. Live preview on the right. No code in sight.

scenes.name: Use cases
scenes.text: What it is good for. [pause] Quick quizzes. Exit tickets. Learning games. Flashcards. Resource hubs. Anything small, focused, and interactive.

scenes.name: Workflow
scenes.text: Four moves. Same loop every time.

scenes.name: First prompt
scenes.text: Be specific. [pause] Who uses it. On what device. What they should do. How feedback works. The clearer the brief — the closer version one lands.

scenes.name: Visual edit
scenes.text: <emphasis>Visual edit</emphasis> mode lets you click any element and ask for a change. [pause] Make the start button green. Bigger font. The AI handles the code.

scenes.name: Publish & test
scenes.text: Click publish — Lovable gives you a shareable link. [pause] Always test on the school network and the actual student device <emphasis>before</emphasis> the lesson.

scenes.name: Privacy
scenes.text: Lovable is for adults. [pause] Teachers build. Students just open the published link. No real student data inside the app. Ever.
